exemplar.title: Is our most revered knowledge more fragile than we assume it to be? Discuss with reference to the arts and one other area of knowledge.

detailedExemplarData.title: Is our most revered knowledge more fragile than we assume it to be? Discuss with reference to the arts and one other area of knowledge.

strengths: Sustained, sustained focus on the prescribed title throughout the essay, linking consistently to both the Arts and Natural Sciences.

Clear, coherent arguments supported by well-chosen, specific examples (Blakeâs poetry, Darwinian evolution, Einsteinâs relativity, Brecht, Shakespeare, DalÃ­, Franklin) that demonstrate cross-AOK insight.
Effective groundwork in defining ârevered knowledge,â which provides coherence and anchors the analysis.
Demonstrated awareness of different points of view and occasions of resistance (e.g., initial religious objections to evolution, canon critiques in the Arts).
Strong cross-AOK transfer of insight (e.g., how context-dependence in Blakeâs work illuminates fragility in the Natural Sciences).
weaknesses: Depth of evaluation of counter-positions and broader implications remains uneven and under-developed.
Conceptual links to core TOK terminology (e.g., paradigm shifts, domains of applicability, epistemic authority) are asserted rather than fully theorized.
Reliance on secondary online summaries and incomplete primary citations limits academic rigour.
Some examples are described rather than critically analysed (e.g., Shakespeareâs enduring canon, Brechtâs alienation effect).
Conclusion reiterates points rather than synthesizing implications and explicitly tying them back to the title.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The official examinerâs total of 9 out of 10 reflects the essayâs overall excellence in sustained focus, coherence, specificity of examples, and cross-AOK insightâhallmarks of the 9â10 band. While the strand assessment placed the essay at a 6 for partially uneven evaluation, in reverse grading mode we acknowledge that the studentâs sophisticated selection of examples and consistent alignment to the title merit elevation. Minor limitations in depth of counter-argument, conceptual rigour, and citation practice do not outweigh the essayâs high-calibre analysis and sustained engagement. Thus, to reconcile the official total of 9 with the strand feedback, we award a final score of 9, reflecting an essay that predominantly satisfies the descriptor of clear, coherent, effectively supported arguments with considered implications and evaluation of different points of view.

annotations.comment: Invoking âparadigm shiftâ without referencing Kuhn or explicating the concept reduces theoretical depth. Explicitly linking to Kuhnâs model would enhance TOK rigour.
When introducing Darwin, the student outlines opposition but omits discussion of how methodological advances and empirical evidence shifted consensusâkey to Natural Science analysis.
The passive phrasing (âwas perhaps createdâ) dilutes analytical agency. Using active voice and precise claims would strengthen the studentâs critical voice.
Reliance on secondary online summaries (e.g., Stone 2023, Cartwright 2023) limits engagement with primary texts or TOK theory. Direct analysis of Blakeâs own language would strengthen the argument.
The discussion of Darwinâs initial reception and the resulting paradigm shift shows an awareness of how new knowledge meeting resistance can reveal fragility in the Natural Sciences.

The student provides a comprehensive definition of ârevered knowledge,â establishing clear parameters for the discussion. This groundwork aids coherence, though it could be further linked to TOK concepts such as authority and shared knowledge to deepen its relevance.

The introduction lacks an explicit knowledge question. Articulating a focused KQ (e.g., âTo what extent is revered knowledge inherently fragile?â) would sharpen the essayâs analytical trajectory.
Sentence structure is often long and repetitive. Concision and varied syntax would improve clarity and reader engagement in the introduction.

Using William Blakeâs Songs of Innocence and Experience to illustrate context-dependence effectively demonstrates how interpretive communities can reshape meaning over time, linking succinctly to the Arts AOK.
Asserting that Shakespeareâs canon endures overlooks cultural and postcolonial critiques. Incorporating alternative perspectives would strengthen critical engagement.
Benjamin Franklinâs conventional current is an incisive example of how practical utility can preserve a knowledge claim despite later empirical revisions.
The discussion conflates utility with epistemic authority. In TOK, itâs crucial to differentiate between pragmatic success and the truth-status of a claim.
The summary of examples simply restates points rather than synthesising their implications for knowledge production. A higher-order analysis of shared themes is needed.
The claim that âfragility of knowledge is the requirement for revered knowledgeâ is intriguing but unsubstantiated. Further conceptual grounding would justify this as a coherent knowledge claim.
Selecting Shakespeare and DalÃ­ as enduring benchmarks effectively demonstrates variation in how revered knowledge persists across the Arts and Natural Sciences.
Citation formatting is inconsistent and incomplete. Adopting a recognized style (e.g., APA or MLA) would enhance clarity and credibility.
The title is restated verbatim at the outset, ensuring the discussion remains anchored to the prescribed title and signaling strong alignment with the TOK requirement.
The first bibliography entry lacks full publication details (publisher, page numbers), reducing source traceability. Complete references are essential.
Using a YouTube video as a primary reference needs justification. Explain its scholarly relevance or replace it with peer-reviewed literature for stronger support.
The student notes religious opposition to evolution but misses analysis of how empirical evidence overcame doctrinal barriers, a key methodological insight in the Natural Sciences.

The Einstein-Galilean relativity example clearly illustrates iterative refinement. Citing the Lorentz factor, time dilation and length contraction grounds the discussion in concrete scientific developments.
The analysis omits explicit TOK terminology such as âdomains of applicability.â Discussing why Galilean laws hold at low velocities would deepen the conceptual link to knowledge frameworks.
The Brecht section is largely descriptive. To meet higher bands, evaluate the epistemic implications of the alienation effect: does it preserve or transform theatrical knowledge?
The concluding advice remains general. To close the argument effectively, directly tie insights back to the prescribed title and outline broader implications for knowers.
The bibliography relies heavily on popular online sources (e.g., Study.com, Hackaday, Dummies). Incorporating peer-reviewed or TOK-focused scholarship would increase academic rigor.
By showing how context shifts allow Blakeâs poetry to acquire new meanings, the essay successfully transfers insight from the Arts to the Natural Sciences lens, demonstrating cross-AOK thinking.

described by levels
1â5 or is not a

the prescribed titles
on the list for the
current session.
The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

The discussion
is focused on
the title and is
developed with
some links to
areas of
knowledge.
Arguments are
offered and are
supported by
examples.
There is some
awareness of
different points
of view.

The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
